volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Truculentus (Section 2.3.326 - Section 2.4.407b)

sections.label: Section 2.3.326
sections.translation: Can ’ t you endure waiting for a short time even ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.327
sections.original: Quin hercle lassus iam sum durando miser :
sections.translation: Why , ’ pon my faith , I ’ m wretchedly tired with waiting already .

sections.label: Section 2.3.328
sections.original: mihi quoque prae lassitudine opus est ut lavem .
sections.translation: I , as well , shall be obliged to go bathe from weariness

sections.label: Section 2.3.329
sections.original: sed obsecro hercle , Astaphium , i intro ac nuntia
sections.translation: But , i ’ faith , Astaphium , prithee do go in-doors and tell her

sections.label: Section 2.3.330
sections.original: me adesse , ut properet suade , iam ut satis laverit .
sections.translation: that I ’ m here . Do go at once , and persuade her that she has bathed long enough by this .

sections.label: Section 2.3.331
sections.translation: Very well .

sections.label: Section 2.3.331b
sections.translation: And do you hear as well ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.331c
sections.translation: What do you want ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.331d
sections.translation: May the Gods confound me for calling you back . I had nothing to say to you , only do be off .

sections.label: Section 2.3.333
sections.original: Quidnam revocabas ? improbu'simprobus es nihilique homo :
sections.translation: Why did you call me back then , you worthless and good-for-nothing fellow ? A delay to me which has produced fully a mile ’ s delay to you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.335
sections.original: Sed quid haec hic autem tam diu ante aedis stetit ?
sections.translation: But yet why was she standing here so long before the house ? Some one , I don ’ t know who , she certainly was waiting for ; the Captain , I suppose . That ’ s it ; see now , how , just like vultures [*] , a whole three days beforehand they foresee on what day they are to have a feast . They ’ re all agape for him ; on him are all their minds fixed .

sections.label: Section 2.3.340
sections.original: me nemo magis respiciet , ubi is est huc venerit ,
sections.translation: No one will be giving any more attention to myself , when he comes , than if I had been dead two hundred years ago . How delightful a thing it is to keep one ’ s money ! Ah wretched me ! after it ’ s done I ’ m punished , who lost what I once had .

sections.label: Section 2.3.344
sections.original: verum nunc si qua mi obtigerit hereditas
sections.translation: But now , if any great and splendid fortune should chance to fall to my lot , now , after I know it , what sweets and what bitters come of money , by my troth , I ’ d so keep it , I ’ d live in a manner so sparing , that — in a few days I ’ d make there to be none at all . I ’ d then confute those who now censure me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.350
sections.translation: But I perceive that this tide-like door is opening ( the door of PHRONESIUM ’ S house is opened ) , which sucks up whatever comes within its bolts .

sections.label: Section 2.4.352
sections.original: Num tibi nam , amabo , ianua est mordax mea ,
sections.translation: Please now , is my door apt to bite [*] , that you are afraid to come in , my love ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.353b
sections.translation: Behold the spring ! How all blooming it is ! how fragrantly does it smell ! how brightly does it shine .

sections.label: Section 2.4.355
sections.original: Quid tam inficetu'sinficetus es , Lemno adveniens qui tuae
sections.translation: Why so ill-mannered , as not , on your arrival from Lemnos [*] , to give a kiss to your mistress , my Dinarchus ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.357
sections.original: Vah , vapulo hercle ego nunc , atque adeo male .
sections.translation: O dear , by my troth , I ’ m being punished now , and most terribly —

sections.label: Section 2.4.358
sections.translation: Why do you turn yourself away ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.358b
sections.translation: My greetings to you , Phronesium .

sections.label: Section 2.4.359
sections.original: Salve . hicine hodie cenas , salvos cum advenis ?
sections.translation: Greetings to you as well . Will you dine here to-day , as you ’ ve arrived in safety ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.360
sections.translation: I ’ m engaged .

sections.label: Section 2.4.360b
sections.translation: Where will you dine then ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.360c
sections.translation: Wherever you request me ; here .

sections.label: Section 2.4.361
sections.translation: You ’ ll give me pleasure by doing so .

sections.label: Section 2.4.361b
sections.translation: I ’ troth , myself still more . You ’ ll give me your company to-day , I suppose , my Phronesium ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.363
sections.translation: If it could possibly be done , I would .

sections.label: Section 2.4.363b
sections.translation: Give me my shoes [*] then — make haste , remove the table .

sections.label: Section 2.4.364b
sections.translation: Are you in your senses , pray ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.365
sections.original: Non edepol bibere possum iam , ita animo malest .
sections.translation: By heavens , I cannot drink now ; so sick at heart am I .

sections.label: Section 2.4.366
sections.translation: Stay ; something shall be done . Don ’ t go .

sections.label: Section 2.4.366b
sections.translation: Ah , you ’ ve refreshed me with cold water ! My senses have now returned . Take off my shoes [*] ; give me something to drink .

sections.label: Section 2.4.368
sections.original: Idem es mecastor qui soles . sed dic mihi ,
sections.translation: By my faith , you are just the same that you used to be . But tell me , have you sped successfully ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.369b
sections.original: Huc quidem hercle ad te bene ,
sections.translation: I ’ troth , successfully enough , indeed , hither to you , inasmuch as I enjoy the opportunity of seeing you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.370b
sections.translation: Embrace me then .

sections.label: Section 2.4.371
sections.original: Lubens . heia , hoc est melle dulci dulcius .
sections.translation: With pleasure . ( He embraces her .) Oh , this is honey sweeter than sweet honey . In this , Jove , my fortune does exceed thine own !

sections.label: Section 2.4.373
sections.translation: Won ’ t you give me a kiss ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.373b
sections.translation: Aye , ten even .

sections.label: Section 2.4.373c
sections.translation: You are not niggardly in that . You promise more than I ask of you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.375
sections.original: Vtinam a principio reí item parsisses meae
sections.translation: I only wish that from the first I had been as sparing of my property , as you are now thrifty of your kisses .

sections.label: Section 2.4.376b
sections.translation: If I could possibly cause you any saving , i ’ troth , I could wish it done .

sections.label: Section 2.4.378
sections.translation: Have you bathed then ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.378b
sections.original: Iam pol mihi quidem atque oculis meis .
sections.translation: I ’ troth , indeed I have then , to my own satisfaction and that of my eyes . Do I seem to be loathsome to you ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.379b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , not to myself indeed ;

sections.label: Section 2.4.380
sections.original: verum tempestas , memini , quondam etiam fuit ,
sections.translation: but I remember that there was once a time when between ourselves we were loathsome [*] , the one to the other . But what doing of yours is this I ’ ve heard upon my arrival ? What new matter have you been scheming here in my absence ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.384
sections.translation: Why , what is it ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.384b
sections.original: Primumdum , cum tu es aucta liberis
sections.translation: In the first place , that you ’ ve been blessed with children ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.385
sections.translation: and that you ’ ve safely got over it , I ’ m delighted .

sections.label: Section 2.4.386
sections.original: Concedite hinc vos intro atque operite ostium .
sections.translation: Go you away from there into the house , and shut the door . ( They go in , and shut the door .) You now alone are left to be present at my communication ; to you I ’ ve ever entrusted my designs . For my own part , I ’ ve neither had any child nor have I been pregnant ;

sections.label: Section 2.4.390
sections.original: verum adsimulavi me esse praegnatem : haud nego .
sections.translation: but I ’ ve pretended that I was pregnant ; I wasn ’ t though .

sections.label: Section 2.4.391
sections.translation: For what reason , O my life ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.391b
sections.translation: On account of a Babylonian Captain , who kept me as though his wife for a year , while he was here .

sections.label: Section 2.4.393b
sections.translation: That I knew . But what means this ? For what purpose was your design in pretending this ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.395
sections.original: Vt esset aliquis laqueus et redimiculum ,
sections.translation: That there might be a certain bond and tie [*] for him to be returning to me again . Now he has lately sent me a letter hither , that he ’ ll make trial how much I value him . If I should raise and bring up the child which I should bear ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.400
sections.original: bona sua med habiturum omnia esse .
sections.translation: that then I should have all his property .

sections.label: Section 2.4.400b
sections.translation: I listen with pleasure . In fine , what is it you are contriving ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.401b
sections.translation: My mother ordered the servant-maids , since now the tenth month is arriving close at hand , each to go in some different direction , to seek out and bespeak a boy or a girl , to be passed off as my own .

sections.label: Section 2.4.405
sections.original: quid multa verba faciam ? tonstricem Suram
sections.translation: Why need I make many words ? You know Syra , the female hair-dresser [*] , who now lives hard by our house ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.407
sections.translation: I know her .

sections.label: Section 2.4.407b
sections.original: Haec ut opera circumit , per familias ,
sections.translation: She , with the utmost care , went about among the families , and secretly found out a child , and brought it to me . She said it was given to her .
